element_introduction_order.element: Vehicle selection (Ferrari 328 Twin Turbo or F1 Machine)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Both vehicles perform identically; choice is cosmetic
Fundamental controls established before time pressure escalates
Time management becomes central tension mechanic; checkered flags signal progress
Turbo unlocks higher speed potential; requires resource management through time pressure
Crashing causes vehicle flip; escalates difficulty as competitor speed increases

element_introduction_order.element: Competitor AI vehicles (Volkswagen Beetles → Ferrari Testarossas)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Competitor speed increases with each stage; introduces racing tension

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Each course features distinct location (Los Angeles, San Francisco, Athens, etc.)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Visual feedback communicates course progression; no gameplay impact

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Optional at any time during play
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Optional accessibility feature; enhances sense of speed perception

element_introduction_order.element: Dashboard HUD (speedometer, tachometer, score, time remaining)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Constant feedback loop; time remaining drives tension

typical_relief_triggers: Passing checkered flag checkpoint (adds time or signals stage completion)
Coasting deceleration on time-out (5-10 second grace period before failure)
typical_tension_triggers: Competitor vehicles increasing in speed across stages
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive escalation across 8 stages; early courses establish patterns with abundant time, later courses compress time windows and increase AI competitor speed; tension builds continuously within each stage via visible countdown clock

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden level select accessible via vehicle select screen (press B to increment courses 0-7, hold Up+Right, press Start)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Generous time windows; low competitor speed (Volkswagen Beetles); establishes control patterns and checkpoint timing
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Time compression increases; competitor variety and speed escalates; obstacles become more frequent
difficulty_curve.late_game: Tight time windows; high-speed competitors (Ferrari Testarossas); collision avoidance demands precision steering
notable_spikes: Course 3 (Grand Canyon) - environmental variation increases visual complexity
Course 7 (Seaway in Typhoon) - thematic hazard environment with aggressive weather visual effects
Courses 6-8 - marked escalation in competitor speed and checkpoint spacing

notable_level_patterns.description: Eight-stage arc from West Coast (Los Angeles, San Francisco, Sunset Coastline) through central USA (Grand Canyon) to East Coast; thematic variation in skylines and environments reinforces narrative of 'Transamerica' race progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Course 1 (Sunset Coastline) → Course 5 (Los Angeles Night Way) → Course 8 (Last Seaside Running)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage subdivided by checkered flag checkpoints that grant additional time or signal stage completion; checkpoints create distinct micro-tension cycles within longer stage progression; unlike Out Run, coasting deceleration on timeout grants 5-10 second grace period

Each course features distinct visual identity (snow, typhoon, urban night, ancient ruins) without mechanically altering gameplay; visual feedback communicates progression without requiring player adaptation to new rules
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Course 6 (Snow White Line) vs Course 7 (Seaway in Typhoon) vs Course 4 (Ruins of Athens)

notable_level_patterns.description: Opponent vehicles progress from economical (Volkswagen Beetles) in early courses to high-performance (Ferrari Testarossas) in late courses; speed increase parallels time window compression to maintain consistent challenge difficulty
